authorDescription: Born in 1998 · Dual master's graduate in demography and computer science, interested in building solutions to support open science in the field of demography and quantitative longitudinal surveys.

jobs.occupation: Conception, creation and management of IT tools for demographic surveys
jobs.description: Within INED's Survey and Polling Department, conceiving, developing and testing software tools dedicated to the production of metadata and the creation of demographic surveys, monitoring and promoting the DDI standard, maintaining and developing the DataIned website, liaising with various partners regarding data availability, and co-developing dedicated solutions for metadata.

Within the INED DataLab and GGP Central Hub team, responsible for making metadata available across all participating countries, contributing to Central Hub coordination, DDI standard advocacy, building CI pipelines to automate production and testing, and maintaining the GGP contextual database.
Inventory and valorisation of demographic data, best practice documentation, API analysis and documentation. Creation and documentation of SCSS styleguide.
Analysis, design and development of fullstack e-commerce solutions. Managing integrations between ERP, CRM, PIM and e-commerce platforms via REST and GraphQL APIs.
Development of internal web applications including image processing, machine learning pipelines, user database management and student onboarding interfaces.
Analysis and development of a Dialogflow chatbot to answer employee HR questions within a consulting firm.

education.field: IUT Robert Schumann, University of Strasbourg

education.degree: University Diploma of Technology · Computer Science
education.field: IUT Robert Schumann, University of Strasbourg

education.mention: Mathematics · European German section · Latin option

publications.title: L'étude internationale GGP, les enquêtes Erfi et leur documentation au standard DDI
publications.description: Presentation illustrating efforts to document and standardise GGP programme surveys, with a focus on the French Erfi 2 survey.

publications.title: Processing cross-national longitudinal panel surveys to document rich metadata using automation and open standards
publications.description: This work focuses on the automation and application of open standards to improve the processing of cross-national longitudinal panel surveys.

publications.title: Making Data Comparable: Lessons learned from the Generations and Gender Programme
publications.venue: Ritzenthaler, T., Grünwald, O. · Censuses, surveys and data harmonisation in Europe
publications.description: Advances in GGP data harmonisation: centralised coordination, metadata processing and FAIR principles for open science.

publications.title: How comparable are the cross-national surveys in the Generations and Gender Survey Round II?
publications.venue: Connolly, A., Ritzenthaler, T., Grünwald, O., Caporali, A. · EPC 2024
publications.description: Examination of compliance to the baseline questionnaire in GGS-II and whether comparability has improved compared to round one.
